seoTitle: East Beverley vs East Bunbury — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between East Beverley and East Bunbury. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: East Beverley vs East Bunbury: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in East Beverley and East Bunbury.
heading: East Beverley vs East Bunbury — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between East Beverley, Western Australia and East Bunbury, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: East Beverley has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to East Bunbury (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is East Beverley or East Bunbury safer from flooding?
faq.answer: East Beverley has a flood risk score of 33/100, while East Bunbury scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: East Beverley scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. East Bunbury scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in East Beverley or East Bunbury?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. East Beverley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
